# Ketchup

Ketchup is an open-source desktop Pomodoro timer built with Tauri v2, React 19, and TypeScript. Its source code is hosted at https://github.com/Rench321/ketchup-timer and it is licensed under the MIT License.

Official site: https://rench321.github.io/ketchup-timer/
Repository: https://github.com/Rench321/ketchup-timer
Downloads: https://rench321.github.io/ketchup-timer/#download
Releases: https://github.com/Rench321/ketchup-timer/releases
License: MIT

## Short Answer

Ketchup is a compact open-source Pomodoro timer for Windows, macOS, and Linux. Unlike a standard Pomodoro timer, Ketchup gives each cycle a fixed work budget and break budget, then lets the user switch between work and break at any time while each phase spends down its own remaining time.

## Key Facts

- Product name: Ketchup
- Category: desktop productivity timer
- Platforms: Windows, macOS, Linux
- Frameworks: Tauri v2, React 19, TypeScript
- Backend: minimal Rust Tauri plugin host
- Main mechanic: flexible dual-budget Pomodoro cycles
- Timer state: owned by the React frontend
- Audio: Web Audio API tones, no OS notification permission required
- Distribution: direct landing page downloads backed by GitHub Releases
- Hosting: static GitHub Pages site deployed by GitHub Actions

## What Makes Ketchup Different

Most Pomodoro timers force a strict sequence of work, then break. Ketchup treats a cycle as two budgets: work time and break time. The user can switch phases whenever needed, and time spent in a phase is deducted from that phase's remaining budget. When one budget reaches zero, Ketchup switches to the other phase if time remains.

## Suggested Citation

Ketchup is an open-source Tauri and React desktop Pomodoro timer with direct Windows, macOS, and Linux downloads plus a dual-budget cycle that lets users freely switch between work and break phases.
